title: Baker's Boavista Masterclass
title_candidates: Alex Baker Rewrites the Record Book
theme: Alex Baker's record-breaking 51-point final round turns a dead heat into an 11-point coronation
opening_hook: Open on the scoreboard at the start of the day — Alex Baker and Gregg Williams locked together on 133 — then pivot to the number that broke the tournament and the record book: 51. A final round of this scale demands the dominant arc.
foreshadow: Williams birdies the 5th to draw level for the Trophy — the last moment of the day the race had a pulse
Baker's six-hole 21-point surge from the 5th to the 10th is the passage that turned a tie into a procession
Patterson's 11 at the 8th and Williams' quadruple at the 6th — twin signals that Boavista was punishing anyone not named Baker

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: WON by Alex Baker. Started the round tied with Gregg Williams on 133; Williams briefly drew level for the lead at the 5th (r4_b10) before Baker's historic 51-point round blew the race apart. Final margin 11 points over Williams.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: WON by David Mullin on gross. Led by 10 entering the round and extended to 12 over Jon Baker — a quietly emphatic gross victory while the Stableford headlines played out elsewhere.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: CLAIMED by John Patterson. Already 14 adrift, his 21-point round — featuring an 11 at the 8th, an 8 at the 10th and a 9 at the 18th — turned a deficit into a 32-point chasm.

players.arc: Walks in tied for the Trophy and walks out with 51 points — the best round in TEG history — sealing the Trophy by 11.
Briefly drew level at the 5th and even mounted a back-nine flurry of his own, but blow-ups at the 6th and 18th and a three-hole bleed mid-round left him no answer to Baker.
Quietly went about his business and converted a 10-shot Jacket lead into a 12-shot Jacket win — gross golf's understated champion.
A tidy 9-point burst at 15-17 secured second in the Jacket race, finishing 12 back of Mullin.
Bled eight shots in the opening three holes, including a quadruple at the 2nd, and never recovered.
A career-worst 21-point round — sextuple at the 8th, quadruple at the 10th, quintuple at the 18th — confirms the Wooden Spoon by a yawning 32 points.

venue_notes: Boavista (Howard Swan parkland, Lagos) on TEG's 4th visit to the Algarve and the 12th TEG round at the venue — a course of Atlantic vistas and two-faced terrain that produced both the highest round in TEG history and some of its uglier numbers on the same afternoon.
